ocr.text: The Asia-Pacific semiconductor market is undergoing a transformative phase characterized by significant investments in
advanced packaging technologies. As companies navigate the equipment spending cycle, it is essential to analyze capital
expenditures and market share within this evolving landscape. For instance, in 2021, the capital expenditure for advanced
packaging reached ¥30B, demonstrating a strategic commitment to innovation, while the market share associated with this
expenditure was 34%. This upward trend is further emphasized by a growth rate of 6% observed in the sector, reflecting
increasing demand for advanced packaging solutions. Notably, expenditures related to more traditional approaches also saw
substantial investment, with a capital expenditure of ¥6B, which has been projected to grow at a rate of 20%.
Year  UnitsSold  Revenue(CNY)  R&DInvestment  Headcount  NumberofFacilities
GrowthRate  20%  15%  [HIDDEN]  5%  [HIDDEN]
MarketShare  35%  [HIDDEN]  32%  31%  30%
CapitalExpenditure  ¥10B  ¥12B  ¥15B  ¥18B  ¥20B
These figures indicate a robust interest in both established and emerging technologies, as companies seek to enhance their
competitive positioning. Furthermore, the adoption rates of advanced packaging technologies illustrate a broader shift, with an
increase from 15% in 2020 to an anticipated 25%.
Year  Category  AdoptionRate  Investment(CNY)  MarketShare  Growth(%)
2020  AdvancedPackaging  15%  1.2B  25%  N/A
2021  AdvancedPackaging  20%  1.5B  30%  33.3%
2022  AdvancedPackaging  [HIDDEN]  2.0B  35%  25%
2020  3DPackaging  10%  800M  [HIDDEN]  N/A
This dynamic environment underscores the critical need for stakeholders to align their strategies with ongoing trends in
equipment spending, ensuring they remain at the forefront of technological advancement within the semiconductor and
electronics industry.
